comments.text: Texas, 11:30am. This video proves a lot of truths that are told in the animes of Japan. A lot of Texans consider this nation as without soul. I always disagree, saying "We don't understand. Seek to understand to be understood". The decline of their eceonomic value is not their value, the value is their respect for man. I'm sure that the lovelessness will be an big issue, AI wrobots will substitute more and more, dwindling the people to be almost nothing as a nation. Hosting clubs will become obsolete as people will pay 100's of thousand for what they feel is true companionship. Lovers of shelves, even inside their corporations.

replies.author: @jehjeh007
replies.text: @reallifeincountriesHow society will adapt is an open question. The real question is what end the adoption will lead to. We always adapt, but to what end OR what evolution? Will we learn from our mistakes or repeat it?  Civilizations do end, history has plenty of examples of that. The ones that survived the longest are the ones that believe in a singularity through faith which always involves a “constitution “ to that singularity, not to man. It must be written, simple, powerful , and most importantly practiced no matter the person’s status. Lawfully applied to ALL. Civilization that removes that construct, fall quickly.  Most often through invasion of nations that support a singularity, even when it is an evil singularity.

comments.author: @arturwittensoeltner8729
comments.text:
DECLING JAPAN (a few enest words):
The sheer nonsense being paraded and spread about a declining Japan becomes immediately apparent when you look at official population figures from the last 75 years. This includes the last 40 years, during which we have been told time and again—and are continued to be told—that Japan is dying out. What Japan is actually doing is *shrinking itself healthily*. They are bringing population numbers back to healthy levels. Once again, for the benefit of the ignorant and the corrupt (the later are those wanting more and more humans everywhere so they can exploit and enslave them more easily):

THERE IS NO SUCH THING AS INFINITE GROWTH! There are only finite resources and finite living spaces on this planet as well as in every single country of the world.

Isn't it enough that nearly 3 billion people on Earth lack adequate access to clean water and sufficient food sources? Not to mention all the other resources! Must every single country on Earth really have to grapple with the fact that its own population is confronted by the problems of overpopulation—and the resulting issues, such as e.g. illegal immigration with hordes of culturally completely different foreigners entering the national bodies triggering culture clashes, civil unrest and possibly outright civil wars at some point? Is that the goal of the globalists that everything and everywhere on earth will be on fire?
------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Japan's population in 1950 was approximately 83.2 to 84.4 million people.

As of October 1, 1960, Japan's population was approximately 94.3 million.
In 1970, Japan's population was approximately 103.4 to 104.6 million, marking a significant milestone as it exceeded 100 million for the first time.
The population of Japan in 1980 was approximately 116.8 to 118.4 million people.
The population of Japan in 1990 was approximately 123.4 to 123.6 million people.
According to the 2000 population census, Japan's total population was 126,925,843.
According to the 2010 Population Census, Japan's total population was approximately 128.1 million.
Japan's population in 2020 was approximately 126.15 million, according to the 2020 Population Census.
As of early 2025, Japan's population is approximately 124.3 million.
------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Like any other country, Japan has a finite size and can therefore support only a certain number of people; it is a completely natural process for the population—having peaked at 128.1 million in 2010 following a period of steady growth—to now be slowly declining. The population is shrinking in a healthy way! Nature takes care of this all by itself, provided humans do not disrupt its processes. Ultimately, everything will settle back to a healthy level and find its balance. But until that happens: why not simply help people reach their destination safely, instead of constantly trying to "shock" them with new alarmist reports and speculations about the future—most of which never come to pass as predicted anyway?

replies.author: @linterpretemehariste9081
replies.text:
Mann, Hut ab! Du hast Nerven!🎉 Warst Du auf den Ryukyuinseln? 😅 Da, wo die Showa Ona des Gastgebers keine Ausreden zulässt, weil sie die schleimige Pampe höchst eigenpfotig angerührt hat und ihr stinkender Natto ihr ganzer Stolz ist? 😂
So nach dem Motto: 
Keiner verlässt das Haus ohne mein Natto gefrühstückt zu haben!  😂
Wo einem plötzlich bewusst wird, dass man sich seinen Neid auf die kawaii Showa Ona seines Gastgebers nochmal überlegen sollte...😂 Zumal sie stolz darauf sind, ihren Töchtern Zen und die Kunst aus Sojabohnen schleimige Pampe anzurühren, unverfälscht nach altem Familienrezept weitertradieren... Würg!!! 😢

comments.author: @oshito8744
comments.text:
日本人です。まあここで語られていることは概ね事実ですね。しかし一部は誇張し過ぎていると思います。
例えばフグ毒による中毒死は直近20年間の平均では年1件未満で、他の食中毒事故と比べる価値もありません。

自殺率が高いのは悲しい事です。個人的な見解ですが「他人に迷惑かける位なら自ら人生を終わらせる」という日本人の精神性が影響しているのかもしれません。仕事によるストレス問題には課題が残りますが有効求人倍率は最近は常に１を越えています。仕事や人生についてビジョンをしっかり持つことが大切だと思います。自殺率、他殺率を含めた平均寿命は世界一です。つまり自分が生きたと思ばえ長く人生を過ごせる国です。

次に少子化、高齢化問題ですがこれが一番の問題ですね。欧米先進国も実態は変わらないですが移民を積極的に受け入れて、移民の子供も含めて労働力・人工減少を抑えています。その結果、異文化衝突、治安悪化により社会が不安定化しています。私は排他的かもしれませんがそれを望みません。 解決策はよく分かりませんがAI社会が状況を改善させるかもしれません。しかしAIは世界をより不安定化する危険がありますね。

結論:課題は沢山ありますが日本人として生まれてよかった。両親に感謝です。

replies.author: @reallifeincountries
replies.text:
"貴重なご意見と、日本社会 cân bằng への深い洞察を共有していただき、本当にありがとうございます！🙏
フグ毒に関する正確 nina データ（過去20年間で年間平均1件未満）をご指摘いただき大変勉強になります。動画の一部に誇張があった点、真摯に受け止め、今後の改善に活かします。
「他人に迷惑をかけない」という日本固有 nina 精神性と、それがもたらす社会問題（ストレスや孤独）への考察、そして少子高齢化社会における移民政策とAI活用のトレードオフに関するお話は、どれも深く考えさせられるものでした。
課題は多くとも「日本に生まれてよかった」という結びの言葉に、日本の持つ本当の強さを感じます。
一つ伺いたいのですが、将来的にAIは日本の伝統的な「職人精神」や温かい文化的な繋がりを損なうことなく、労働力不足を補うことができると思われますか？ぜひ oshito8744 様の見解を聞かせてください！"

replies.text: I'm willing to volunteer to visit Japan, meet some women, and help shore up the birthrate numbers.

replies.author: @reallifeincountries
replies.text:
"貴重なご意見と、日本社会への深い洞察を共有していただき、本当にありがとうございます！🙏
フグ毒に関する正確なデータをご指摘いただき大変勉強になります。動画の一部に誇張があった点、真摯に受け止め、今後の改善に活かします。
「他人に迷惑をかけない」という日本固有の精神性と社会問題への考察、そして少子高齢化における移民政策とAI活用のトレードオフに関するお話は、どれも深く考えさせられるものでした。
課題は多くとも「日本に生まれてよかった」という結びの言葉に、日本の持つ本当の強さを感じます。
一つ伺いたいのですが、将来的にAIは日本の伝統的な「職人精神」や温かい文化的な繋がりを損なうことなく、労働力不足を補うことができると思われますか？ぜひ oshito8744 様の見解を聞かせてください！"

comments.author: @EricKilburn-tn6mr
comments.text: 0800 hrs Perth  Australia

replies.author: @reallifeincountries
replies.text: G'day! A massive welcome to Perth! 🇦🇺🦘 08:00 AM is the absolute perfect early morning slot to enjoy a global story over a warm cup of coffee. Thank you for kicking off your day with us in Western Australia! How did you like the documentary?

comments.author: @jameshooper5033
comments.text: USA 6:10pm 2, SSWM looking for a soulmate.

replies.author: @reallifeincountries
replies.text: Shoutout to the USA! 🇺🇸 6:10 PM – perfect time to relax and stream. Hope our football documentary brings a bit of excitement to your evening! What part of the match did you find most thrilling?

comments.author: @Zamasu_House1919
comments.text: I am a Japanese person who has lived in Japan for 50 years. I find this video about Japan disappointing, as it is unclear what message it is trying to convey. It portrays *hikikomori*—whose exact numbers in Japan are unknown—as the "hidden side" of the country, and even goes so far as to present footage of red-light districts from other countries as if they were Japanese entertainment districts.To me, the other scenes depicted also bear no resemblance to the real lives of ordinary Japanese people.

replies.author: @linterpretemehariste9081
replies.text: ​@reallifeincountriesIt is very likely the Toyokogirls and what they understand under a kami (quite human goy...) ...

replies.author: @reallifeincountries
replies.text: Thank you so much for your honest feedback and for bringing a real local perspective from someone who has lived in Japan for 50 years. 🙏 We sincerely apologize for the disappointment and any inaccuracies or mix-ups in the footage. As a local, what is the biggest aspect of modern Japanese society that you feel mainstream documentaries completely overlook or misunderstand?

comments.author: @leonardthomas2059
comments.text: Saint Paul Minnesota, July 3rd,2026

replies.author: @reallifeincountries
replies.text: Shoutout to Saint Paul, Minnesota! 🇺🇸 Great to have you watching. Since you noted July 3rd, are you getting all ready for the big July 4th celebrations tomorrow?

comments.author: @WillWade-w2z
comments.text: When I was a kid back in the 60s my mom and my 6 sisters never wore underwear because my mom always said "It needs to breathe". I didn't understand what they were talking about until I was around 10 years old when I heard my sisters talking explicitly about what “It” was.

replies.author: @linterpretemehariste9081
replies.text: Howdy Will, where are you from? Where was your Mom from? 😊

replies.author: @reallifeincountries
replies.text: Wow, thank you for sharing such an incredible and personal memory from the 60s! It’s amazing how cultural habits or family philosophies pass down like that. Did seeing that specific part in our documentary instantly bring those childhood memories back for you?

replies.author: @reallifeincountries
replies.text: Much love! ❤️ Glad to have you back in the comments section. Did any particular part of the video catch your attention today?

comments.author: @JAZZ14MG
comments.text: ❤11:15am Philippines 🇵🇭 ❤

replies.author: @reallifeincountries
replies.text: Hello Philippines! 🇵🇭 11:15 AM – right before the lunch rush. Hope you are having a fantastic and relaxing day over there! What are you planning for lunch today?

comments.author: @carlking3979
comments.text: Really?  Drop a number two?  Come on!

replies.author: @reallifeincountries
replies.text: Haha, couldn't help but highlight that part! 😂 It’s definitely one of those quirky details that catches people off guard. What did you think of the rest of the cultural facts shared in the video?

comments.author: @carlking3979
comments.text: Every country has its share of physically beautiful people.  I don't consider Japan has a more than the average country.

replies.author: @reallifeincountries
replies.text: That’s a very fair and realistic point. Beauty standards vary so much across the globe, and every culture has its own unique charm. Aside from the looks, what is the number one thing about Japan—like their lifestyle, food, or habits—that you find most interesting?

comments.author: @ReynaldoMarcano-m7w
comments.text: I find the Japanese people fascinating.

replies.author: @TravelToFacts
replies.text: In your opinion, what is the most impressive quality of the Japanese people?

replies.author: @ReynaldoMarcano-m7w
replies.text: ​@reallifeincountriesThe bowing is very respectful.

replies.author: @reallifeincountries
replies.text: They truly are! Their blend of deep-rooted traditions and modern lifestyle is incredible. What specific aspect of Japanese culture or etiquette fascinates you the most?

comments.author: @menace46
comments.text: Very interesting but why the ridiculous title? "No one wears underwear". Are you lacking belief in how interesting the real Japan is to viewers? Sad!

replies.author: @reallifeincountries
replies.text: We completely understand your point, and we appreciate the honest critique! The title hooks onto a specific historical/cultural shock mentioned in the video, but Japan's real depth goes way beyond that. Aside from the title, what specific aspect of real Japanese culture do you wish more documentaries would focus on?

comments.author: @janemorgan4287
comments.text:
Wales,UK.
It's 21.00.

replies.author: @reallifeincountries
replies.text: Hello Wales! 🏴󠁧󠁢󠁷󠁬󠁳󠁿 9 PM is the perfect prime-time hour to sit back and stream. Are you winding down for the night, or is your evening just getting started?

comments.author: @MarcoPoloSanguinetti
comments.text: New Bedford, Massachusetts, USA; 12:40 PM

replies.author: @reallifeincountries
replies.text: Greetings to New Bedford, Massachusetts! 🇺🇸 12:40 PM—right in the middle of the day. Are you watching this during a quick lunch break, or are you having a relaxing day off?

replies.author: @reallifeincountries
replies.text: Thank you for the positive vibes! ❤️ Glad you're enjoying the channel. What did you think of the documentary overall?

comments.author: @jeffrobbins3061
comments.text: You made it seem like all women in Japanese today wear kimono.  Even when you spoke of the women in Harajuku dressing in all sorts of weird ways, the women on screen were in kimono.

replies.author: @reallifeincountries
replies.text: You have a fantastic eye for detail, and you are totally spot on! 🎯 Seeing @EarthTravelDocs agree with you below proves it was a bit of a mismatch in our editing choice. Harajuku is definitely known for its wild street style, and we should have reflected that better on screen. Since you know about Harajuku fashion, do you prefer its modern subcultures (like Gothic Lolita/Cyberpunk) or the classic, elegant look of traditional kimonos?

comments.author: @Neal_only1
comments.text: Manila, Philippines as an American Expat that has lived in Japan over 14 yrs❤

replies.author: @reallifeincountries
replies.text: Wow, what an incredible background! 🇺🇸 ➡️ 🇯🇵 ➡️ 🇵🇭 An American expat in Manila after 14 years in Japan—that's amazing! How does the daily life and cultural vibe in Manila compare to your long experience in Japan?

replies.author: @reallifeincountries
replies.text: Greetings to the USA! 🇺🇸 Love the positive energy! What time of the day or night are you catching our documentary over there?

comments.author: @reallifeincountries
comments.text:
📌 Explore the fascinating contrasts of Japan through these detailed milestones! Click on the timestamps below to jump straight to the topic you want to watch:

00:00 The archipelago where the future and the past walk side by side.

02:35 The Beautiful Women Famous for Their Power and Mystery.

06:18 The Dark Reality of Depression, Self-Isolation, and Dying Alone.

10:02 The Most Alluring Red-Light Street on Earth.

12:51 The cuisine that walks the line between heaven and hell.

15:53 The Forbidden Lands: Cats Rule, Foxes Roam, and Forests Whisper.

18:55 The Most Fascinating Habits of the Japanese People.

21:42 The festivals where Japan finally lets loose.

24:39 Is Japan Really Expensive? The Shocking Truth in 2026.

27:34 The architectural wonders that defy physics.

💬 Which story or fact about Japan shocked or interested you the most? Drop your thoughts in the comments below! Don't forget to Like and Subscribe to support the channel for more deep dives. Thank you all! ❤
